Items.Title: 距离 CodeLand 2022 不到三周 🤯 | CodeLand 2022 is less than three weeks away 🤯
Items.Description:
你好，亲爱的社区！

您可能知道， CodeLand 。 我们非常兴奋地为您带来为期两天的虚拟灵感、动力和学习！

多年来，CodeLand 取得了长足的进步，其历史上的每一章都经过精心设计，以满足当时不断发展的社区的需求。 对于 2022 年，您将在该计划中看到特别关注 Web 可访问性——因为这正是 CodeNewbie 和 DEV 社区所要求的！ 我们的目标是取悦🌈

门票 0 美元起

今年的 CodeLand 有很多令人兴奋的事情，以至于很难选择“最令人兴奋”的功能，但我们一直听到的一件特别棒的事情是，该活动将再次按你的意愿付费——起价为 0 美元. 我们认为，为刚开始职业生涯的全球观众提供一个以各种方式都可以访问的会议非常重要。 处于那个阶段可能意味着资金通常很紧张，我们希望在您所在的地方与大家见面。

惊人的扬声器

如果没有业内知名人士和我们鼓舞人心的社区成员的精心策划的演讲，CodeLand 将一事无成。

今年，您将听到主旨演讲人Angie Jones （倡导各级开发人员和 IBM Master Inventor）、 Kelsey Hightower （有影响力的云计算和开源领导者）和Tracy Chou （在线安全的先驱和倡导者）和反骚扰工具，更不用说时代杂志 2022 年度女性奖得主了）。 您还将听到来自 CodeNewbie 社区的 15 位出色的个人，他们有相关的故事和指导与您分享。

注册加入我们

为了加入 CodeLand 并获得完整的虚拟体验、有资格获得奖品和赠品并参与活动，您需要注册。

我们期待在我们的“嘉宾名单”上看到您的名字，并期待您出席今年的活动💜



原文:

Cross-Posted from CodeNewbie Community.

Hello, dear community!

As you might know, CodeLand. -our annual conference for early-career software developers (and their champions)- is coming up on June 16 & 17, 2022. We couldn't be more excited to bring you what's sure to be two days of virtual inspiration, motivation, and learning!

Over the years, CodeLand has evolved a great deal and each chapter in its history was carefully designed to fit the needs of our growing community at the time. For 2022, you'll see in the program that there's a particular focus on web accessibility — because that's what the CodeNewbie and DEV communities asked for! We aim to please 🌈

Tickets start at $0

There's so much to get excited about at CodeLand this year that it's hard to pick a "most exciting" feature, but one thing we keep hearing is particularly awesome is that the event will be pay-what-you-want again — starting at $0. We believe it's important to deliver a conference that's accessible in every way for our global audience of folks just starting out in their careers. Being at that stage can mean that money is often tight, and we want to meet you all where you're at.

Amazing speakers

CodeLand would be nothing without our highly curated talks from prominent figures in the industry and our inspiring community members alike.

This year, you'll get to hear from keynoters Angie Jones (advocate for developers at every level and IBM Master Inventor), Kelsey Hightower (influential cloud computing and open source leader), and Tracy Chou (a pioneer for and advocate of online safety and anti-harassment tooling, not to mention a Time Magazine 2022 Women of the Year award winner). You'll also hear from 15 amazing individuals from the CodeNewbie Community who have relevant stories and guidance to share with you.

>> Browse the CodeLand Program

Register to join us

In order to join us at CodeLand and get the full virtual experience, be eligible for prizes and giveaways, and participate in activities, you'll need to register.

>> Register for CodeLand

We look forward to seeing your name on our "guest list" and your presence at this year's event 💜

Items.Title: 50+ 提示 JS(ES6+) 开发者必须知道（第 5 部分） | 50+ hints JS(ES6+) developer must know (5th part)
Items.Description:
暴风雨天赋

发表于大家好！ 你好吗？ 这里又是 StormyTalent 来解释 50 多个关键的 JS 提示。

今天我们将了解模块、属性、变量。

始终在非标准模块系统上使用模块（导入/导出）。 您始终可以转换为您喜欢的模块系统。

模块

const numbers = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]; // bad let sum = 0; for (let num of numbers) { sum += num; } sum === 15; // good let sum = 0; numbers.forEach((num) => { sum += num; }); sum === 15; // best (use the functional force) const sum = numbers.reduce((total, num) => total + num, 0); sum === 15; // bad const increasedByOne = []; for (let i = 0; i < numbers.length; i++) { increasedByOne.push(numbers[i] + 1); } // good const increasedByOne = []; numbers.forEach((num) => { increasedByOne.push(num + 1); }); // best (keeping it functional) const increasedByOne = numbers.map((num) => num + 1);

如果您必须使用生成器，或者您无视我们的建议，请确保它们的函数签名间隔正确。

 // bad function * foo() { // ... } // bad const bar = function * () { // ... }; // bad const baz = function *() { // ... }; // bad const quux = function*() { // ... }; // bad function*foo() { // ... } // bad function *foo() { // ... } // very bad function * foo() { // ... } // very bad const wat = function * () { // ... }; // good function* foo() { // ... } // good const foo = function* () { // ... };

特性

访问属性时使用点表示法

const luke = { jedi: true, age: 28, }; // bad const isJedi = luke['jedi']; // good const isJedi = luke.jedi;

访问带有变量的属性时使用括号表示法 []。

 const luke = { jedi: true, age: 28, }; function getProp(prop) { return luke[prop]; } const isJedi = getProp('jedi');

计算幂时使用幂运算符**。

 // bad const binary = Math.pow(2, 10); // good const binary = 2 ** 10;

变量

始终使用 const 或 let 来声明变量。 不这样做会导致全局变量。 我们希望避免污染全局命名空间。 星球船长警告过我们这一点。

 // bad superPower = new SuperPower(); // good const superPower = new SuperPower();

每个变量或赋值使用一个 const 或 let 声明。

 // bad const items = getItems(), goSportsTeam = true, dragonball = 'z'; // bad // (compare to above, and try to spot the mistake) const items = getItems(), goSportsTeam = true; dragonball = 'z'; // good const items = getItems(); const goSportsTeam = true; const dragonball = 'z';

将所有 const 分组，然后将所有 let 分组。

 // bad let i, len, dragonball, items = getItems(), goSportsTeam = true; // bad let i; const items = getItems(); let dragonball; const goSportsTeam = true; let len; // good const goSportsTeam = true; const items = getItems(); let dragonball; let i; let length;

在你需要的地方分配变量，但把它们放在一个合理的地方。

 // bad - unnecessary function call function checkName(hasName) { const name = getName(); if (hasName === 'test') { return false; } if (name === 'test') { this.setName(''); return false; } return name; } // good function checkName(hasName) { if (hasName === 'test') { return false; } const name = getName(); if (name === 'test') { this.setName(''); return false; } return name; }

不要链接变量赋值。

 // bad (function example() { // JavaScript interprets this as // let a = ( b = ( c = 1 ) ); // The let keyword only applies to variable a; variables b and c become // global variables. let a = b = c = 1; }()); console.log(a); // throws ReferenceError console.log(b); // 1 console.log(c); // 1 // good (function example() { let a = 1; let b = a; let c = a; }()); console.log(a); // throws ReferenceError console.log(b); // throws ReferenceError console.log(c); // throws ReferenceError // the same applies for `const`

避免使用一元递增和递减 (++, --)

 // bad const array = [1, 2, 3]; let num = 1; num++; --num; let sum = 0; let truthyCount = 0; for (let i = 0; i < array.length; i++) { let value = array[i]; sum += value; if (value) { truthyCount++; } } // good const array = [1, 2, 3]; let num = 1; num += 1; num -= 1; const sum = array.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0); const truthyCount = array.filter(Boolean).length;

避免在赋值之前或之后的换行符。 如果您的分配违反了 max-len，请将值括在括号中。

 // bad const foo = superLongLongLongLongLongLongLongLongFunctionName(); // bad const foo = 'superLongLongLongLongLongLongLongLongString'; // good const foo = ( superLongLongLongLongLongLongLongLongFunctionName() ); // good const foo = 'superLongLongLongLongLongLongLongLongString';

禁止未使用的变量。

 // bad var some_unused_var = 42; // Write-only variables are not considered as used. var y = 10; y = 5; // A read for a modification of itself is not considered as used. var z = 0; z = z + 1; // Unused function arguments. function getX(x, y) { return x; } // good function getXPlusY(x, y) { return x + y; } var x = 1; var y = a + 2; alert(getXPlusY(x, y)); // 'type' is ignored even if unused because it has a rest property sibling. // This is a form of extracting an object that omits the specified keys. var { type, ...coords } = data; // 'coords' is now the 'data' object without its 'type' property.

谢谢你的时间。



原文:

StormyTalent

Posted on May 28

Hi, everyone! How are you doing? Here is StormyTalent again to explain about the 50+ critical JS hints.

Today we're gonna see about Modules, Properties, Variables.

Always use modules (import/export) over a non-standard module system. You can always transpile to your preferred module system.

Modules

const numbers = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5];

// bad
let sum = 0;
for (let num of numbers) {
  sum += num;
}
sum === 15;

// good
let sum = 0;
numbers.forEach((num) => {
  sum += num;
});
sum === 15;

// best (use the functional force)
const sum = numbers.reduce((total, num) => total + num, 0);
sum === 15;

// bad
const increasedByOne = [];
for (let i = 0; i < numbers.length; i++) {
  increasedByOne.push(numbers[i] + 1);
}

// good
const increasedByOne = [];
numbers.forEach((num) => {
  increasedByOne.push(num + 1);
});

// best (keeping it functional)
const increasedByOne = numbers.map((num) => num + 1);

If you must use generators, or if you disregard our advice, make sure their function signature is spaced properly.

// bad
function * foo() {
  // ...
}

// bad
const bar = function * () {
  // ...
};

// bad
const baz = function *() {
  // ...
};

// bad
const quux = function*() {
  // ...
};

// bad
function*foo() {
  // ...
}

// bad
function *foo() {
  // ...
}

// very bad
function
*
foo() {
  // ...
}

// very bad
const wat = function
*
() {
  // ...
};

// good
function* foo() {
  // ...
}

// good
const foo = function* () {
  // ...
};

Properties

Use dot notation when accessing properties

const luke = {
  jedi: true,
  age: 28,
};

// bad
const isJedi = luke['jedi'];

// good
const isJedi = luke.jedi;

Use bracket notation [] when accessing properties with a variable.

const luke = {
  jedi: true,
  age: 28,
};

function getProp(prop) {
  return luke[prop];
}

const isJedi = getProp('jedi');

Use exponentiation operator ** when calculating exponentiations.

// bad
const binary = Math.pow(2, 10);

// good
const binary = 2 ** 10;

Variables

Always use const or let to declare variables. Not doing so will result in global variables. We want to avoid polluting the global namespace. Captain Planet warned us of that.

// bad
superPower = new SuperPower();

// good
const superPower = new SuperPower();

Use one const or let declaration per variable or assignment.

// bad
const items = getItems(),
    goSportsTeam = true,
    dragonball = 'z';

// bad
// (compare to above, and try to spot the mistake)
const items = getItems(),
    goSportsTeam = true;
    dragonball = 'z';

// good
const items = getItems();
const goSportsTeam = true;
const dragonball = 'z';

Group all your consts and then group all your lets.

// bad
let i, len, dragonball,
    items = getItems(),
    goSportsTeam = true;

// bad
let i;
const items = getItems();
let dragonball;
const goSportsTeam = true;
let len;

// good
const goSportsTeam = true;
const items = getItems();
let dragonball;
let i;
let length;

Assign variables where you need them, but place them in a reasonable place.

// bad - unnecessary function call
function checkName(hasName) {
  const name = getName();

  if (hasName === 'test') {
    return false;
  }

  if (name === 'test') {
    this.setName('');
    return false;
  }

  return name;
}

// good
function checkName(hasName) {
  if (hasName === 'test') {
    return false;
  }

  const name = getName();

  if (name === 'test') {
    this.setName('');
    return false;
  }

  return name;
}

Don’t chain variable assignments.

// bad
(function example() {
  // JavaScript interprets this as
  // let a = ( b = ( c = 1 ) );
  // The let keyword only applies to variable a; variables b and c become
  // global variables.
  let a = b = c = 1;
}());

console.log(a); // throws ReferenceError
console.log(b); // 1
console.log(c); // 1

// good
(function example() {
  let a = 1;
  let b = a;
  let c = a;
}());

console.log(a); // throws ReferenceError
console.log(b); // throws ReferenceError
console.log(c); // throws ReferenceError

// the same applies for `const`

Avoid using unary increments and decrements (++, --)

// bad

const array = [1, 2, 3];
let num = 1;
num++;
--num;

let sum = 0;
let truthyCount = 0;
for (let i = 0; i < array.length; i++) {
  let value = array[i];
  sum += value;
  if (value) {
    truthyCount++;
  }
}

// good

const array = [1, 2, 3];
let num = 1;
num += 1;
num -= 1;

const sum = array.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
const truthyCount = array.filter(Boolean).length;

Avoid linebreaks before or after = in an assignment. If your assignment violates max-len, surround the value in parens.

// bad
const foo =
  superLongLongLongLongLongLongLongLongFunctionName();

// bad
const foo
  = 'superLongLongLongLongLongLongLongLongString';

// good
const foo = (
  superLongLongLongLongLongLongLongLongFunctionName()
);

// good
const foo = 'superLongLongLongLongLongLongLongLongString';

Disallow unused variables.

// bad

var some_unused_var = 42;

// Write-only variables are not considered as used.
var y = 10;
y = 5;

// A read for a modification of itself is not considered as used.
var z = 0;
z = z + 1;

// Unused function arguments.
function getX(x, y) {
    return x;
}

// good

function getXPlusY(x, y) {
  return x + y;
}

var x = 1;
var y = a + 2;

alert(getXPlusY(x, y));

// 'type' is ignored even if unused because it has a rest property sibling.
// This is a form of extracting an object that omits the specified keys.
var { type, ...coords } = data;
// 'coords' is now the 'data' object without its 'type' property.

Thanks for your time.

Items.Title: 针头、干草堆和早期职业工作资源 | Needles, Haystacks and Early Career Job Resources
Items.Description:
很久以前，你可以在 Monster.com 上投简历，然后寄希望于最好的结果。 我在 2000 年代初以这种方式获得了面试机会。 我会从招聘人员那里收到一封随机电子邮件，然后从那里开始。 我的第一次“严肃”技术面试是在 IBM。 我在 IBM 兼职从事技术支持工作，并收到了招聘人员的电子邮件：

招聘人员：你想在 IBM 工作吗？
我：我已经做到了！

谈论纯粹的机会。 但这确实导致了我的第一次现场面试和第一个软件工作机会。
值得庆幸的是， Handshake和其他公司的存在是为了帮助人们从学校毕业或开始他们的职业生涯。

现在我已经深入我的软件职业生涯并且已经担任了几年的招聘经理，我的❤️ 向那些试图获得他们的第一份工作的人表示感谢。 申请许多工作、很少得到反馈和大量等待的感觉一定像是大海捞针。

我帮助建立了一个学徒计划，并指导人们从支持角色过渡到工程师角色。 我试图在他们职业生涯的早期指导或指导人们。 我没有答案，但我有一些答案。 在过去的几个月里，我一直想编制一份资源清单，以帮助寻找技术职位的早期职业人士。 就在这里。

归功于所有这些精彩内容的创造者 - 组织支持社区的人，如VirtualCoffee.io和The Collab Lab ， Danny Thompson等等。 非常感谢virtualcoffee.io中的人们编制了这份清单。

早期软件职业资源

我将这些分类为Job Hunt 、 Community和Learning 。 有什么要补充或认为应该以不同的方式组织吗？ 我有什么不对吗？ 回复并让我知道，我会调整。

求职

用于找到工作、寻找空缺职位、面试等的资源。

社区

如果你想走得快，就一个人走。 想走远，就一起走。

作为一名经验丰富的工程师，我在找工作时需要帮助，我认为获得支持、鼓励和指导很重要。 希望这些社区中的一个可以在您寻找职位时为您提供帮助。

  • 这里有一个职业导师和职业青年频道，这是一个很好的资源。
  • 如果您也有兴趣，请参与并提供帮助！
  • 协作实验室——我只听说过关于这个小组的惊人事情。 当您学习时，“在完全远程的环境中与您的同龄人一起工作的地方”是一个绝妙的主意，它为人们提供了进入全职远程职位的丰富经验。

学习

其他一切。 开始您的软件职业生涯、获得第一个角色的指南、更多关于面试的文章等。



原文:

Photo by Zhifei Zhou on Unsplash

Long ago, you could throw a resume on Monster.com and hope for the best. I landed interviews that way in the early 2000s. I'd get a random email from a recruiter and go from there. (The other was in person career fairs, which I didn't mind but they never led to much for me.)

My first "serious" tech interview was at IBM. I was working at IBM part time in technical support and got an email from a recruiter:

Recruiter: Would you like to work at IBM?
Me: I already do!

Talk about pure chance. But that did lead to my first onsite interview and first software job offer.
Thankfully, Handshake and other companies exist to help folks coming out of school or starting their career.

Now that I'm deep into my software career and have been a hiring manager for a few years, my ❤️ goes out to folks trying to land their first gig. It must feel like a needle-in-a-haystack approach of applying to many jobs, rarely getting feedback, and lots of waiting.

(Shameless plug: if you are looking for a new job after starting your career, I'm hiring folks with 2+ years of experience for SRE and Software Developer roles at NinjaCat. Check us out!)

I have helped build an apprenticeship program and mentored folks transitioning from Support to Engineer roles. I try to mentor or coach folks early in their career. I don't have the answer but I do have some answers. In the last few months, I've wanted to compile a list of resources to help early career folks looking for technical roles. And here it is.

Credit goes to the creators of all this great content - the folks organizing supportive communities like VirtualCoffee.io and The Collab Lab, Danny Thompson, and more. And big thanks to the folks in virtualcoffee.io for compiling this list.

Early Software Career Resources

I'm categorizing these as Job Hunt, Community and Learning. Have anything to add or think it should be organized different? Did I get anything wrong? Respond and let me know and I'll adjust.

Job Hunt

Resources for landing a job, finding open roles, interviewing, etc.

Community

If you want to go fast, go alone. If you want to go far, go together.

I've needed help in my job search as an experienced engineer and I think it's important to have support, encouragement, and guidance. Hopefully one of these communities can help you as you're searching for a role.

  • Denver Devs Discord - My hometown team, if you will. There's a Career Mentors and Career Juniors channel in here that are a good resource.
  • VirtualCoffee.io - One of the most friendly and sincere communities of developers and tech-adjacent folks on the web. Get involved and help out if you're interested too!
  • The Collab Lab - I have only heard amazing things about this group. "A place to work alongside your peers in a fully remote setting" as you learn is an excellent idea and gives folks great experience going into a full time remote position.

Learning

Everything else. Guides to starting your software career, landing your first role, more articles on interviewing, etc.

Items.Title: 用不到 15 行代码创建一个 Markdoc 插件 | Create a Markdoc plugin in less than 15 lines of code
Items.Description:
几周前，我们开源了Markdoc ，这是为Stripe 文档提供支持的创作工具。 为了配合发布，我们发布了一篇博客文章，展示了如何使用@markdoc/next.js 插件开始使用带有 Next.js 的 Markdoc。
在这篇博文中，我将向您展示如何创建自己的插件，以我为Parcel编写的插件为例，以便您可以使用 Markdoc。

在我们开始之前，Parcel 是一个零配置构建工具，包括开箱即用的开发服务器、热重载、自动生产优化等。 但是，默认情况下它不解析 Markdown 文件，因此，为了能够将 Markdoc 与 Parcel 一起使用，您需要一个特定的插件。 由于 Markdoc 是 Markdown 的扩展，标准的 Markdown 插件不足以正确解析文件。

如果您想直接跳到完整的插件，请随时查看repo 。

创建插件

根据您要为其构建插件的框架或工具，他们可能会处理他们的插件系统略有不同，但插件中包含的代码应该相对相似。 在其核心，需要做的是调用Markdoc.parse()和Markdoc.transform()方法。

在 Parcel 中，转换资产的插件称为 Transformer。

这是这个 Transformer 的样子：

 import { Transformer } from " @parcel/plugin " ; import Markdoc from " @markdoc/markdoc " ; export default new Transformer ({ async transform ({ asset }) { const code = await asset . getCode (); const ast = Markdoc . parse ( code ); const content = Markdoc . transform ( ast ); asset . type = " js " ; asset . setCode ( `export default ${ JSON . stringify ( content )} ` ); return [ asset ]; }, });

首先，您需要从 Parcel 导入Transformer ，并从 @markdoc/markdoc 导入 Markdoc。

然后，在 Parcel 转换器中访问文件内容的方法是调用asset.getCode() 。 从那里，您必须从 Markdoc 调用.parse()和.transform()方法来处理原始 Markdown 内容。

最后，您需要返回转换后的内容。 然后，您需要调用setCode方法将内容替换为新生成的内容，并将其附加到export default之后。 最后一部分特定于 Parcel，但是，需要调用parse和transform才能为任何其他工具创建插件。

使用插件

要使用此插件，您需要首先在应用程序的.parcelrc文件中粘贴以下行：

 { " extends " : " @parcel/config-default " , " transformers " : { " *.md " : [ " parcel-transformer-markdoc " ] } }

这段代码所做的是扩展 Parcel 的默认配置以处理以*.md结尾的文件并将自定义转换器应用于这些文件的内容。

然后，如果你想象一个带有名为getting-started.md的 Markdown 文件的 React 应用程序，显示此内容所需的代码将如下所示：

 import content from " ./getting-started.md " ; import Markdoc from " @markdoc/markdoc " ; import React from " react " ; export function App () { const html = Markdoc . renderers . react ( content , React ); return ( < main > { html } < /main > ); }

就是这样！ 现在，Markdown 文件将被正确解析，您可以使用 Markdoc 提供的所有功能。

其他插件

开发者社区的其他成员也开始制作自己的插件。 随意查看它们：

  • 如果您最终创建了自己的插件，请告诉我们！

    关于作者

    她喜欢研究和试验技术。 当她不编码时，她喜欢在户外度过时光，尝试新啤酒和阅读。



原文:

A couple of weeks ago, we open-sourced Markdoc, the authoring tool powering the Stripe docs. To accompany the launch, we published a blog post showing how to get started using Markdoc with Next.js, using the @markdoc/next.js plugin.
In this blog post, I’ll show you how to create your own plugin, using the one I wrote for Parcel, as an example, so that you can use Markdoc with it.

Before we get started, Parcel is a zero-configuration build tool that includes a development server, hot reloading, automatic production optimization and more, out of the box. However, it does not parse Markdown files by default so, to be able to use Markdoc with Parcel, you need a specific plugin. As Markdoc is an extension of Markdown, a standard Markdown plugin won’t be enough to parse files properly.

If you want to skip directly to the complete plugin, feel free to check out the repo.

Creating a plugin

Depending on the framework or tool you’d like to build a plugin for, they might handle their plugin system a little differently but the code contained in the plugin should be relatively similar. At its core, what needs to be done is call the Markdoc.parse() and Markdoc.transform() methods.

In Parcel, a plugin that transforms assets is called a Transformer.

Here’s what this Transformer looks like:

import { Transformer } from "@parcel/plugin";
import Markdoc from "@markdoc/markdoc"; export default new Transformer({ async transform({ asset }) { const code = await asset.getCode(); const ast = Markdoc.parse(code); const content = Markdoc.transform(ast); asset.type = "js"; asset.setCode(`export default ${JSON.stringify(content)}`); return [asset]; },
});

To start, you need to import Transformer from Parcel, and Markdoc from @markdoc/markdoc.

Then, the way to access the content of files in a Parcel transformer is to call asset.getCode(). From there, you have to call the .parse() and .transform() methods from Markdoc to handle the raw Markdown content.

Finally, you need to return the transformed content. Markdoc.transform() returns a JSON object, so we need to specify that the type of the content is now JavaScript, by changing the .type property to ‘js’. Then, you need to call the setCode method to replace the content with the newly generated one and append it after export default. This last part is specific to Parcel, however, calling parse and transform is required to create a plugin for any other tool.

Using a plugin

To use this plugin, you need to start by pasting the following lines in the .parcelrc file in your application:

{ "extends": "@parcel/config-default", "transformers": { "*.md": [ "parcel-transformer-markdoc" ] }
}

What this code does is extend Parcel’s default configuration to handle files ending in *.md and applying the custom transformer to the content of these files.

Then, if you imagine a React app with a Markdown file called getting-started.md, the code needed to display this content would look something like this:

import content from "./getting-started.md";
import Markdoc from "@markdoc/markdoc";
import React from "react"; export function App() { const html = Markdoc.renderers.react(content, React); return ( <main> {html} </main>
 );
}

That’s it! Now, Markdown files will be parsed properly and you can use all the features Markdoc provides.

Other plugins

Other members of the developer community have also started making their own plugins. Feel free to check them out:

  • Svelte-markdoc
  • Esbuild-markdoc-plugin
  • Solidjs-markdoc
  • Vue-markdoc

If you end up creating your own plugin, let us know!

Items.Description:
对象下标 que está interesado en uno o más temas del Blog。

拉萨利达：

 John: New article for you, theme Technology Mary: New article for you, theme Programming

Código completo GitHub . Codigo 完成GitHub 。



原文:

Permite a un objeto notificar a otros objetos sobre cambios de su estado.

Para entender este patron de diseño analizaremos el siguiente problema: Imaginemos que tenemos un objeto Blog en el que escribimos sobre temas de Tecnología, Matemáticas y Programación, y un

objeto Subscriptor que está interesado en uno o más temas del Blog.

El subscriptor puede visitar el blog cada un tiempo para comprobar que se halla escrito un artículo de su interés. Pero, mientras no hallan nuevos artículos con el tema de interés para el subscriptor, la mayoría de las visitas del

subscriptor al blog serán innecesarias.

El blog podría enviar cientos de notificaciones a los suscriptores cada vez que haya una nueva publicación, ayudando al suscriptor tener que entrar al blog para comprobar si hay un nuevo artículo pero molestaría a los suscriptores que no le

interesan todos los temas .

El resultado es un conflicto en el que, o el suscriptor tiene que entrar constantemente al blog, o el blog realiza un
uso innecesario de recursos para notificar a los clientes que no desean recibir notificaciones de algunos temas.

Solución utilizando el patrón de diseño Observer:

from abc import ABC, abstractmethod class ISubscriber(ABC): @abstractmethod def notify(self, blog) -> None: pass class IBlog(ABC): @abstractmethod def attach(self, subscriber) -> None: pass @abstractmethod def detach(self, subscriber) -> None: pass @abstractmethod def notifier(self) -> None: pass

2. Creamos el objeto Blog

from typing import List class Blog(IBlog): # Propiedad que va a cambiar cada vez que se publique un nuevo tema
 _last_topic: str = None # Lista de suscriptores(lista de objetos).
 _subscribers: List[ISubscriber] = [] @property def last_topic(self): return self._last_topic @last_topic.setter def last_topic(self, value): # Aquí aprovechamos el setter para que cuando se asigne un valor a la propiedad last_topic notifique a los suscriptores
 self._last_topic = value self.notifier() def attach(self, subscriber: ISubscriber) -> None: # Agregar suscriptor(objeto)
 self._subscribers.append(subscriber) def detach(self, subscriber: ISubscriber) -> None: # Quitar suscriptor(objeto)
 self._subscribers.remove(subscriber) def notifier(self) -> None: # Método para realizar las notificaciones
 for subscriber in self._subscribers: # Se llama al método notify de los suscriptores y se le pasa como parámetro la instancia de la clase Blog.
 subscriber.notify(self)

3. Creamos objeto suscriptor


class Subscriber(ISubscriber): def __init__(self, name: str): self.name = name self._subscribed_topics: List[str] = [] def notify(self, blog: IBlog) -> None: theme = blog.last_topic # Verificamos si el ultimo tema del blog le interesa al suscriptor
 if theme in self._subscribed_topics: # Notificamos con una impresión por consola
 print(f'{self.name}: New article for you, theme {theme}') def add_topic(self, topic: str) -> None: self._subscribed_topics.append(topic) def remove_topic(self, topic: str) -> None: self._subscribed_topics.remove(topic)

Ahora probemos el código anterior:

if __name__ == '__main__': # Creamos un objeto de la clase Blog
 blog = Blog() # Creamos un objeto de la clase Subscriber
 subscriber1 = Subscriber('John') subscriber1.add_topic('Technology') # Suscribimos al tema Tecnología
 subscriber2 = Subscriber('Mary') subscriber2.add_topic('Programming') # Suscribimos al tema Programación
 # Suscribimos al objeto de la clase Subscriber al objeto de la clase Blog
 blog.attach(subscriber1) blog.attach(subscriber2) # Asignamos un valor a la propiedad last_topic de la clase Blog
 blog.last_topic = 'Technology' # Desinscribimos al objeto de la clase Subscriber al objeto de la clase Blog
 blog.detach(subscriber1) # Asignamos un valor a la propiedad last_topic de la clase Blog
 blog.last_topic = 'Programming'

La Salida:

John: New article for you, theme Technology
Mary: New article for you, theme Programming

Items.Title: 我们如何在 Azure 中使用 BYOIP？ | How we can use BYOIP in Azure ?.
Items.Description:
微软获准宣传该系列。 来自自定义 IP 地址前缀的地址可以以与 Azure 拥有的公共 IP 地址前缀相同的方式使用。 来自自定义 IP 地址前缀的地址可以与 Azure 资源相关，与内部/私有 IP 和虚拟网络交互，并从 Azure 广域网出站到达外部目标。

好处

  • 客户可以保留其 IP 范围 (BYOIP) 以保持已建立的声誉并继续跳过外部控制的白名单。
  • 公共 IP 地址前缀和标准 SKU 公共 IP 可以从自定义 IP 地址前缀派生。 这些 IP 的使用方式与 Azure 拥有的公共 IP 相同。

将 IP 前缀引入 Azure

将 IP 前缀引入 Azure 是一种三阶段方法：

  • 验证
  • 条款
  • 委员会

验证

真正引入 Azure 的公共 IP 地址范围需要通过您拥有并在路由 Internet 注册表（如ARIN或RIPE ）中注册。 当你将 IP 范围交付给 Azure 时，它​​仍归你所有。 你应该授权微软宣传这个范围。 您对该范围的所有权及其与您的 Azure 订阅的关联会另外得到验证。 其中一些步骤将在 Azure 后院完成。

条款

完成上述步骤后，公网 IP 范围即可完成 Provisioning 阶段。 该范围将在您的订阅中创建为自定义 IP 前缀资源。 公共 IP 前缀和公共 IP 可以从您的范围派生，并与支持标准 SKU 公共 IP 的任何 Azure 资源相关（从自定义 IP 前缀派生的 IP 还可以通过DDoS Protection Standard得到保护）。 此时可能不会公布 IP 并且无法访问这些 IP。

委员会

准备就绪后，您可以发出命令从 Azure 公布您的范围并进入调试阶段。 该范围将首先从自定义 IP 前缀所在的 Azure 位置进行营销，然后通过微软的广域网 (WAN) 到 Internet。 曾经配置该范围的特定区域将在 Microsoft 的 IP Range GeoLocation 页面上公开发布。

限制

  • 自定义 IP 前缀应该与单个 Azure 区域相关。
  • 自定义 IP 前缀目前不再支持 IPv6。
  • 在具有可用区域的区域中，自定义 IP 前缀应该与区域冗余或分配给唯一区域完全相同。 如果在这些区域中未指定区域，则无法创建它。 前缀中的所有 IP 都应该具有相同的区域属性。
  • 当前不支持通过 Azure ExpressRoute 从自定义 IP 前缀播发 IP。
  • 配置后，无法将自定义 IP 前缀范围移动到任何其他订阅。 无法在单个订阅的资源组内移动自定义 IP 地址前缀范围。 从具有适当权限的另一个订阅中的自定义 IP 前缀派生公共 IP 前缀是可行的。
  • 从自定义 IP 前缀使用的任何 IP 地址目前都依赖于订阅和区域的标准公共 IP 配额。 如有需要，请联系 Azure 协助以延长配额。
  • 带到 Azure 的 IP 当前不能用于 Windows Server 激活。

价钱

配置或使用自定义 IP 前缀是免费的。 任何公共 IP 前缀和从自定义 IP 前缀派生的公共 IP 地址均免费。

所有发往自定义 IP 前缀范围的流量均按 Web 出口费率收费。 从 Azure 内到自定义 IP 前缀地址的客户流量需要为其流量的源区域的 Internet 出口收费。 来自自定义 IP 地址前缀不同的出口流量按与来自同一区域的 Azure 公共 IP 相同的价格收费。

感谢您阅读我的文章直到最后。 我希望你今天学到了一些特别的东西。 如果您喜欢这篇文章，请分享给您的朋友，如果您有建议或想法与我分享，请在评论框中写下。



原文:

A custom IP address prefix is a contiguous range of IP addresses owned by using an external customer and provisioned into a subscription. Microsoft is approved to advertise the range. Addresses from a custom IP address prefix can be used in the equal way as Azure owned public IP address prefixes. Addresses from a custom IP address prefix can be related to Azure resources, interact with internal/private IPs and virtual networks, and attain external destinations outbound from the Azure Wide Area Network.

Benefits

  • Customers can hold their IP ranges (BYOIP) to maintain established reputation and proceed to skip thru externally controlled allowlists.
  • Public IP address prefixes and standard SKU public IPs can be derived from customized IP address prefixes. These IPs can be used in the identical way as Azure owned public IPs.

Bring an IP prefix to Azure

It's a three phase method to bring an IP prefix to Azure:

  • Validation
  • Provision
  • Commission

Validation

A public IP address range it really is introduced to Azure need to be owned through you and registered with a Routing Internet Registry such as ARIN or RIPE. When you deliver an IP range to Azure, it remains beneath your ownership. You ought to authorize Microsoft to advertise the range. Your ownership of the range and its association with your Azure subscription are additionally verified. Some of these steps will be completed backyard of Azure.

Provision

After the previous steps are completed, the public IP range can complete the Provisioning phase. The range will be created as a customized IP prefix resource in your subscription. Public IP prefixes and public IPs can be derived from your range and related to any Azure resource that supports Standard SKU Public IPs (IPs derived from a customized IP prefix can additionally be safeguarded with DDoS Protection Standard). The IPs may not be advertised at this point and not reachable.

Commission

When ready, you can issue the command to have your range advertised from Azure and enter the Commissioning phase. The range will be marketed first from the Azure place where the customized IP prefix is located, and then by Microsoft's Wide Area Network (WAN) to the Internet. The specific region where the range was once provisioned will be posted publicly on Microsoft's IP Range GeoLocation page.

Limitations

  • A custom IP prefix ought to be related with a single Azure region.
  • The minimum size of an IP range is /24.
  • IPv6 is presently no longer supported for custom IP prefixes.
  • In regions with availability zones, a custom IP prefix ought to be exact as either zone-redundant or assigned to a unique zone. It can't be created with no zone specified in these regions. All IPs from the prefix ought to have the equal zonal properties.
  • The advertisements of IPs from a custom IP prefix over Azure ExpressRoute aren't currently supported.
  • Once provisioned, customized IP prefix ranges can't be moved to any other subscription. Custom IP address prefix range can't be moved within resource groups in a single subscription. It's viable to derive a public IP prefix from a customized IP prefix in another subscription with the proper permissions.
  • Any IP addresses utilized from a custom IP prefix presently rely towards the standard public IP quota for a subscription and region. Contact Azure assist to have quotas extended when required.
  • IPs brought to Azure can't currently be used for Windows Server Activation.

Pricing

There is no charge to provision or use custom IP prefixes. There is no cost for any public IP prefixes and public IP addresses that are derived from custom IP prefixes.

All traffic destined to a customized IP prefix range is charged the web egress rate. Customers traffic to a customized IP prefix address from within Azure are charged internet egress for the source region of their traffic. Egress traffic from a custom IP address prefix vary is charged the equal price as an Azure public IP from the identical region.

Thanks for reading my article till end. I hope you learned something special today. If you enjoyed this article then please share to your friends and if you have suggestions or thoughts to share with me then please write in the comment box.

原文:

Qual a diferença entre os dois modificadores?

constant não ocupa espaço no armazenamento do contrato mas só pode ser definido uma vez e somente durante a compilação. Será reservado apenas o espaço necessário no bytecode, limite máximo de 32 bytes.

immutable não ocupa espaço no armazenamento do contrato mas só pode ser definido uma vez e somente durante a construção do contrato. Será reservado exatos 32 bytes no bytecode a cada acesso, mesmo se o tamanho da variável for menor.

Items.Title: SheCodeAfrica 的 2022 年贡献 | Contributhon 2022 by SheCodeAfrica
Items.Description:
该队列的参与指导组织是 CHAOSS、Layer5、Jenkins、Moja Global 和 Coil。

上个月以学员身份加入 Contributionn 后，我被分配到CHAOSS ，这是我第一次为开源做出贡献。 我与一位学员——Iyimide 以及 CHAOSS 的三位导师——Ruth Ikegah、Elizabeth Barron 和 Matt Snell 一起工作。 随着这批贡献的结束，我记录了我在过去几周内的活动摘要。

概述

我与 Iyimide 一起参与了CHAOSS Newcomer Slack Bot 项目。
我们的任务包括：

  • 确定编写 Slack 机器人的最佳框架。
  • 使用 CHAOSS 的 GitHub 存储库来托管机器人的代码。
  • 如果可能，包括对机器人的测试。
  • 在代码中包含有助于下一个维护者了解机器人工作原理的注释。
  • 与 CHAOSS 的导师合作获取机器人内容，并提出自己的想法！
  • 在项目结束时，记录有关安装、维护、添加到机器人内容或故障排除的信息。

我的贡献

在进行研究和测试后，在导师的指导下，我们建立了项目 repo（我们目前仍在维护）并建立 CI/CD。 之后，我们构建并部署了机器人。

完成项目的初始阶段后，我专注于编写文档

  • 挑战

    我最初对这个项目有怀疑，因为它不是我熟悉的东西。 此外，这是我在之前的几次尝试失败后第一次为开源做出贡献，因为这一切都非常令人困惑。 但是，团队很棒。 我从我的导师和我的同伴那里得到了（而且我仍然得到）我需要的所有支持和鼓励。 我的任何困惑很快就被清除了。

    获得的经验

    我在这个项目中学到/改进的一些技能/概念是：

    • 持续集成/持续部署
    • 单元测试
    • 技术写作
    • 使用 Git 进行故障排除
    • 开发者签收
    • 有效沟通

    改进建议

    • 这个队列很棒，如果有更多来自资源丰富的人的介绍，下一个队列会更好。 他们是锦上添花。

    下一步

    虽然我仍在为 CHAOSS 做出贡献，但我也在寻找全职职位和其他开源机会。 我希望将来能成为一名 Contributionn 导师。

    谢谢你，SheCodeAfrica 💗。



原文:

Contributhon is a 2-month Open Source Bootcamp designed by SheCodeAfrica to pair mentors from different global open source organizations with African women in tech who get to work with them on selected projects within the organization. The participating mentoring organizations for this cohort were CHAOSS, Layer5, Jenkins, Moja Global, and Coil.

After joining Contributhon as a mentee last month, I was assigned to CHAOSS, where I contributed to open source for the first time. CHAOSS is an open source project at the Linux Foundation focused on creating analytics and metrics to help define community health.
I worked with a fellow mentee- Iyimide, and three mentors from CHAOSS- Ruth Ikegah, Elizabeth Barron, and Matt Snell. As this cohort of Contributhon comes to an end, I have documented a summary of my activities in the last couple of weeks.

Overview

I worked on the CHAOSS Newcomer Slack Bot Project with Iyimide.
Our tasks included:

  • Determine the best framework for writing the Slack bot.
  • Use CHAOSS’s GitHub repository to host the code for the bot.
  • Include tests for the bot, if possible.
  • Include comments in the code that will help the next maintainer understand how the bot works.
  • Work with mentors at CHAOSS for bot content, and bring your own ideas!
  • At the end of the project, document the information for installing, maintaining, adding to bot content, or troubleshooting.

My Contribution

After doing research and testing, and with some guidance from our mentors, we set up the project repo(which we currently still maintain) and set up CI/CD. Afterward, we built and deployed the bot.

After completing the initial phase of our project, I focused on writing the documentation

  • CHAOSS Slack Bot Documentation

Challenges

I had my doubts about the project initially because it was not something I was familiar with. Also, it was my first time contributing to open-source after several previous attempts which failed because it was all very confusing. However, the team was great. I got (and I'm still getting) all the support and encouragement I needed from my mentors and my co-mentee. Any confusion on my part was quickly cleared up.

Experience gained

Some of the skills/concepts I learned/improved on during this program are:

  • Continuous integration/Continuous Deployment
  • Unit testing
  • Technical writing
  • Troubleshooting with Git
  • Developer sign-off
  • Effective communication

Improvement Suggestions

  • This cohort was great, and the next cohort would be even better with more presentations from resourced persons. They were the icing on the cake.

Next Steps

While I am still contributing to CHAOSS, I am also looking out for full-time roles and other Open Source opportunities. I hope to be a Contributhon mentor in the future.

Thank you, SheCodeAfrica 💗.

Items.Title: 使用 TypeScript 编译器避免项目中出现 TypeScript 错误 | Avoid TypeScript errors in your project by using the TypeScript compiler
Items.Description:
假设你有一个包含很多特性的大型 TypeScript 项目。 在这个项目中，您的任务是重构整个应用程序中最重要的组件之一：用户注册表单（假设您使用的是 React），这是您的应用程序中极其详细且至关重要的功能。

好吧，你已经完成了，你重构了所有东西......现在有很多 TypeScript 错误，因为这个组件接收到的 props 的类型与你应用程序中这个组件的所有用法不同。 您如何轻松找到所有用途？

当然，您可以使用旧的Cmd+Shift+F或Ctrl+Shift+F ，但还有另一种更简单、更好的方法可以做到这一点。

为了解决这个问题，我们可以使用tsc 。 首先，什么是tsc ？

假设你是 TypeScript 的新手， tsc是 TypeScript 编译器。 它是一个负责将您的 TypeScript（它是 JS 的超集）代码转换为纯 JavaScript 的工具。 由于到目前为止，浏览器只理解 JavaScript 代码， tsc使您的 TS 代码可以被浏览器读取。

好的，我们如何使用它？

假设您已经有一个带有tsconfig.json文件和package.json文件的 TypeScript 项目，如果还没有，请添加一个scripts属性。

 ...
"scripts" : { ...
} ,
...

它将在这里我们将添加我们的typecheck命令：

 ...
"scripts" : { "typecheck" : "tsc --project tsconfig.json --noEmit" ,
} ,
...

好的，现在让我们回顾一下这个命令：

  1. 首先，我们执行tsc编译器
  2. 我们传递 --project 标志来明确告诉编译器我们要使用我们指定的配置文件（即 tsconfig.json）对整个项目进行类型检查。
  3. 最后，我们通过 --noEmit 标志表示我们不想将编译后的 JS 文件输出到我们的项目中。 如果我们不设置此标志，例如，我们有一个位于src/utils/boolToText.ts的 TS 文件，则会创建一个名为src/utils/boolToText.js的文件，它是 JavaScript 版本的src/utils/boolToText.ts文件由编译器输出。

如果您的代码中没有 TS 错误，您应该会看到如下内容：

如果发现一些错误，您应该会看到如下内容：

现在您可以打开有错误的文件并修复它们:)

您可以使用的其他配置

我还喜欢做一些额外的配置，以确保我的项目安全可靠，没有任何错误。

使用 husky 和 ​​lint-staged 在 pre-commit 上运行

我喜欢做的一件事是在预提交时使用lint-staged和husky配置我的 typecheck 命令以及我的tests和eslint验证。

所以，我的.lintstagedrc.json文件看起来像这样：

 { "*.{ts,tsx}" : [ "eslint 'src/**' --fix" , "npm run test:staged" ]
}

我的.husky/pre-commit文件如下所示：

 #!/bin/sh . " $( dirname " $0 " ) /_/husky.sh" npx --no-install lint-staged npm run typecheck

这样，每次我提交某些东西时，它都会进行 lint 检查，运行我的测试并检查我的整个项目。

如果这样做，您将获得TS5042: Option 'project' cannot be mixed with source files on a command line. 错误。 在与 lint-staged 分离的 husky pre-commit 钩子中传递npm run typecheck将对整个项目进行类型检查，而不仅仅是暂存文件。 它并不完美，但它有效！ 官方 TypeScript 存储库中有一个关于此的问题，以防您想查看。

如果你还没有安装 lint-staged 和 husky，可以参考他们的官方文档：

在 VSCode 上创建一个任务以监视错误

您可以做的另一件好事是创建一个VSCode 任务，以在您编写代码时监视项目中的错误。

去做这个：

  1. 打开命令面板(Cmd+Shift+P)或(Ctrl+Shift+P) 。
  2. 选择Tasks: Configure Task 。
  3. 选择tsc: watch - tsconfig.json
  4. 确保在 tsconfig.json 的 "compilerOptions" 部分中设置了"noEmit": true选项，以便tsc不会在整个项目中输出js文件。

现在，将在您的项目中创建一个.vscode/tasks.json ，其中包含以下内容：

 { "version" : "2.0.0" , "tasks" : [ { "type" : "typescript" , "tsconfig" : "tsconfig.json" , "option" : "watch" , "problemMatcher" : [ "$tsc-watch" ], "group" : "build" , "label" : "tsc: watch - tsconfig.json" } ]
}
  1. 如果您愿意，可以设置自定义标签。
 { ... "tasks" : [ { ... "label" : "Watch for TypeScript errors" } ]
}
  1. 现在，您可以再次打开命令面板并选择Tasks: Run Task
  2. 选择Watch for TypeScript errors （或您选择的名称）任务。

这将打开一个新的终端窗口来执行你的任务，它会继续观察错误。

恭喜。 您已完成创建 VSCode 任务 :)

结论

恭喜！ 现在您有了一个防错项目，特别是如果您也配置了 husky 和 ​​lint-staged。

一如既往，欢迎改进和/或更正😀。

玩得开心编码！

进一步阅读



原文:

Let's suppose you have a large TypeScript project with a lot of features. In this project, you are tasked of refactoring one of the most important component in your entire application: the user registration form (assuming you are using React), which is an extremely detailed and vitally important feature in your application.

Alright, you have done it, you refactored everything and... now there are a lot of TypeScript errors because the types of the props that this component receives are different from all of the usages of this component in your app. How do you find all your usages easily?

Sure you can use the good old Cmd+Shift+F or Ctrl+Shift+F, but there's another easier and better way to do this.

To fix this, we can use tsc. The good thing about tsc is that you can use it to maintain the integrity of your project and keep it free from errors.

First of all, what is tsc?

Assuming you are new to TypeScript, tsc is The TypeScript Compiler. It is a tool responsible for turning your TypeScript (which is a superset of JS) code into plain JavaScript. Since, as of today, browsers only understand JavaScript code, tsc makes your TS code readable by browsers.

Alright, how do we use it?

Assuming you already have a TypeScript project with a tsconfig.json file and also a package.json file, add a scripts property if there isn't one yet.

...
"scripts": { ...
},
...

It'll be here where we will add our typecheck command:

...
"scripts": { "typecheck": "tsc --project tsconfig.json --noEmit",
},
...

Alright, now let's go over this command:

  1. First, we execute the tsc compiler
  2. We pass the --project flag to explicitly tell the compiler that we want to typecheck the entire project using our specified config file, which is the tsconfig.json.
  3. Lastly, we pass the --noEmit flag to say that we do not want to output the compiled JS files into our project. If we don't set this flag and, for example, we have a TS file located at src/utils/boolToText.ts, there would be created a file called src/utils/boolToText.js, which is the JavaScript version of the src/utils/boolToText.ts file outputed by the compiler.

If there are no TS errors in your code, you should see something like this:

If some error(s) was found, you should see something like this:

Now you can open the files with errors and fix them :)

Additional configuration you can use

There are also some additional configuration I like to do to make sure my project is safe and sound, free from any errors.

Run on pre-commit with husky and lint-staged

One thing I like to do is to configure my typecheck command with lint-staged and husky on pre-commit along with my tests and eslint verification.

So, my .lintstagedrc.json file looks something like this:

{ "*.{ts,tsx}": [ "eslint 'src/**' --fix", "npm run test:staged" ]
}

And my .husky/pre-commit file looks like this:

#!/bin/sh
. "$(dirname "$0")/_/husky.sh" npx --no-install lint-staged
npm run typecheck

This way, every time I commit something, it will do lint check, run my tests and typecheck my entire project.

💡 Notice: The reason why I'm not executing my npm run typecheck inside lint-staged is that, since lint-staged only runs staged files and passes the current file being checked by argument to the command being executed, we can't have a reference to the tsconfig.json file (with the --project flag) and a source file to check in the same command. If you do, you'll get a TS5042: Option 'project' cannot be mixed with source files on a command line. error. Passing npm run typecheck in the husky pre-commit hook separated from lint-staged will typecheck the entire project, and not just the staged files. It's not perfect, but it works! There's an issue in the official TypeScript repository about this, in case you want to take a look.

If you don't have lint-staged and husky installed yet, you can refer to their official documentation:

Create a task on VSCode to watch for errors

Another nice thing you can do is create a VSCode task to watch for errors in your project as you code.

To do this:

  1. Open the command palette (Cmd+Shift+P) or (Ctrl+Shift+P).
  2. Select Tasks: Configure Task.
  3. Select tsc: watch - tsconfig.json
  4. Make sure the "noEmit": true option is set in the "compilerOptions" section in your tsconfig.json so that tsc do not output js files all over your project.

Now, there will be created a .vscode/tasks.json in your project with the following content:

{ "version": "2.0.0", "tasks": [ { "type": "typescript", "tsconfig": "tsconfig.json", "option": "watch", "problemMatcher": [ "$tsc-watch" ], "group": "build", "label": "tsc: watch - tsconfig.json" } ]
}
  1. Set a custom label if your want to. 
{ ... "tasks": [ { ... "label": "Watch for TypeScript errors" } ]
}
  1. Now, you can open your command palette again and select Tasks: Run Task
  2. Select your Watch for TypeScript errors (or the name you chose) task.

This will open a new terminal window with the execution of your task and it will keep watching for errors.

Congratulations. You are done creating your VSCode task :)

Conclusion

Congratulations! Now you have a error-proof project, especially if you configured husky and lint-staged too.

As always, improvements and/or corrections are welcome 😀.

Have fun coding!

Further reading

Items.Title: 了解如何在 Angular 单元测试时单击按钮 | Learn How to Click a Button when Angular Unit Testing
Items.Description:
按钮在用户界面的用户体验中发挥着重要作用。 您是否应该为 Angular 应用程序中的按钮单击提供单元测试用例？ 真的那么重要吗？ 如果是这样，您将如何测试该场景？

在 Angular 中为按钮点击编写单元测试时有两种突出的方法：要么在 DOM 中搜索按钮，执行实际的点击并验证预期的行为，要么只需调用将在按钮被点击。 两种选择都有其优点和缺点。 在本文中，我们将彻底研究每条测试路线并查看各种示例，以便您了解有关如何为 Angular 中的按钮单击编写单元测试所需了解的一切。

为什么以及何时应该对 Angular 中的按钮点击进行单元测试？

如果您有一些自动化测试经验，那么如果您想知道按钮单击是否甚至需要通过 Angular 中的单元测试来处理，这也就不足为奇了。 也许在过去，您选择放弃单元测试并将该责任推迟到 E2E（端到端）测试。 这个决定没有任何问题 - E2E 测试通过模拟应用程序中的真实用户场景，根据用户体验执行测试来验证功能。

另一方面，单元测试更加细化。 它是一段自动调用应用程序中的工作单元（一段单独的代码）的代码，通常从黑盒的角度来处理。 测试通过或失败基于对该工作单元行为的假设或期望。

单元测试几乎总是使用测试框架编写的，因此可以高效地编写并快速运行。 如果您使用 Angular CLI 生成一个新的 Angular 项目，那么您的应用程序会附带开箱即用的 Jasmine 和 Karma（测试框架和运行程序）。

但是，如果您使用另一个测试框架，如 Jest，则方法几乎相同。

关于按钮是否应该被单元测试覆盖并没有固定的规则。 事实上，是否为按钮点击编写单元测试的决定最终归结为个人意见。 如果您更愿意将该功能推迟到 E2E 测试，那就太好了！ 但在我看来，在某些情况下，按钮单击单元测试可以为 Angular 应用程序提供有价值的保证。

考虑一个经典的计算器示例，它包含许多执行各种数学运算的按钮。 每次单击按钮时，都会处理数据并在屏幕上显示新的数字或总和。 这是单元测试的完美场景！ 每次点击按钮，数据都会发生变化； 当给定特定输入时，计算器会产生特定输出。

另一方面，按钮将用户导航到不同的页面，或者使其他内容出现或消失的情况并不少见。 这些场景不仅仅是更改数据，而是代表应用程序功能，并且是编写 E2E 测试的绝佳机会。

考虑到这一点，您的情况是需要进行单元测试还是最好创建 E2E 测试？

回想一下，为按钮编写单元测试通常有两种方法：要么在 DOM 上找到按钮并模拟单击，要么针对用户单击按钮时将运行的代码进行测试。 让我们先看一个更复杂的例子。

如何在 Angular 中测试按钮点击

这种方法在某些情况下可能很有用，但是委派一个单元测试来浏览 DOM 以找到按钮并执行点击的行为是一个争论点。 单元测试仍然围绕单击按钮时应该发生什么的概念提出期望，但许多人认为执行单击是 E2E 测试的责任。

无论如何，在 DOM 上定位按钮是一项微不足道的任务，尤其是当您将按钮隔离为可重用组件时。 下面是一个示例 - 一个可重用的隔离按钮组件，如前所述，假设您已使用 Jasmine 和 Karma 正确配置了 TestBed。

 describe ( ' Component: Button ' , () => { let fixture : ComponentFixture < ButtonComponent > ; let component : ButtonComponent ; beforeEach ( async (() => { TestBed . configureTestingModule ({ imports : [ ], declarations : [ ButtonComponent ], providers : [ ] }). compileComponents (). then (() => { fixture = TestBed . createComponent ( ButtonComponent ); component = fixture . componentInstance ; }); })); }); it ( ' should call onButtonClick when clicked ' , fakeAsync (() => { spyOn ( component , ' onButtonClick ' ); let button = fixture . debugElement . nativeElement . querySelector ( ' button ' ); button . click (); tick (); expect ( component . onButtonClick ). toHaveBeenCalled (); }));

此按钮组件的 TypeScript 文件有一个名为onButtonClick的函数，该函数绑定到模板中的button元素。 该测试首先监视本地函数，找到按钮，然后执行单击。 在使用tick()模拟一段时间后，我们断言调用了onButtonClick函数。

请注意，按钮位于 DOM 上，使用querySelector并将button作为参数传递。 这在像这样的孤立组件中工作正常，但在可能存在多个button元素的不同场景中，您需要使用提供更多特异性的东西。

这个例子非常简单——我们只是验证当按钮被点击时该函数被调用。 但我们可以更进一步。 让我们看看onButtonClick函数，看看还有什么可以测试的。

 @ Output () buttonClicked : EventEmitter < any > = new EventEmitter < any > (); ... onButtonClick (): void { this . buttonClicked . emit (); }

由于这是一个可重用的按钮组件，因此将功能的责任委托给使用它的任何组件是有意义的。 为了让父组件识别按钮何时被点击，它可以监听按钮组件内部的事件发射器（ buttonClicked ）。 响应发出的事件，父组件调用本地函数，例如，执行上面计算器示例中的数学运算。

从测试的角度来看，它提供了一个单元测试的价值，以确保在调用onButtonClick函数时发出事件。 这可能是这样的。

 it ( ' should emit the event when #onButtonClicked is called ' , () => { const emitSpy = spyOn ( component . buttonClickEvent , ' emit ' ); component . onButtonClick (); expect ( emitSpy ). toHaveBeenCalled (); });

间谍再次来到这里救援。 其他情况呢？

在可重用按钮组件中可能存在其他情况，其中单元测试可能被证明是有用的，并确保它在未来通过额外的更改继续工作。 不过，我们不会在本文中讨论或涵盖这些场景。

如何在 Angular 中测试按钮功能

现在可重用按钮组件有一个支持测试用例，让我们将注意力转向测试连接到该按钮组件的本地代码可能被证明有益的情况。

回想一下，可重用按钮组件在单击时会发出一个事件。 我们应用程序的其他部分可以监听该事件，并调用本地函数来执行隔离操作。

继续我们之前的计算器想法，这里有一个示例，我们使用可重用按钮组件并监听buttonClickEvent 。

 <app-button (buttonClickEvent)= "add(5)" > 5 </app-button>

我们已经有在 DOM 上定位按钮并启动点击事件的单元测试，因此无需在父组件中进行测试。 相反，让我们直接看一下add函数，看看里面是否有什么值得测试的地方。

 add ( toAdd : number ) { this . total += toAdd ; }

这是专门针对本文的一个非常基本且直接的示例。 这个函数会改变数据，如果你之前记得，这是一个添加支持测试用例的好机会。

但是你测试什么？

对于add函数，我们将编写一个测试，以确保total类变量随着传递给函数的适当值而递增。 这个例子很简单，但是确定要测试什么的技巧是随着练习而来的。

这是测试的样子。 这再次假设您使用 TestBed 正确设置了测试套件。

 it ( ' should add 5 to the calculator total ' , () => { const expectedTotal : number = 10 ; component . total = 5 ; component . add ( 5 ); expect ( component . total ). toEqual ( expectedTotal ); });

请注意，我们直接在父组件测试中调用了add函数。 请记住，我们已经确保按钮在单击时按预期工作，因此在这种情况下，我们只需调用被测代码。

我建议在add函数（而不是测试）中故意出错，以查看测试是否在需要失败时失败。

结论

在本文中，我们研究了在 Angular 中测试按钮点击的不同方法。 一种选择是编写一个单元测试来定位 DOM 上的按钮，执行单击并断言测试中发生了某些事情。 另一方面，简单地调用在单击按钮时调用的函数并根据被测代码中发生的情况编写预期可能是合适的。

最终，这取决于您的个人喜好。 无论您选择采取哪种方式，我希望这篇文章对您有所帮助，并向您展示如何为 Angular 中的按钮点击编写单元测试。



原文:

Buttons play a large part in the user experience of your user interface. Angular makes working with buttons extremely easy, but perhaps you’ve hit a wall when your mindset switches to testing. Should you have unit test cases for button clicks in your Angular application? Is it really that important? And if so, how would you go about testing that scenario?

There are two prominent approaches when it comes to writing unit tests for button clicks in Angular: either you search the DOM for the button, perform an actual click and verify the expected behavior, or you simply call the component-code that will run when the button is clicked. Both options have their pros and cons. In this article, we’ll investigate each testing route thoroughly and look at various examples so that you understand everything you need to know about how to write unit tests for button clicks in Angular.

Why and when should you unit test button clicks in Angular?

If you have some experience with automated testing, it wouldn’t be surprising if you’re wondering if a button click is something that even needs to be handled with a unit test in Angular. Perhaps in the past you’ve opted to forgo a unit test and defer that responsibility to an E2E (End-to-End) test. There isn’t anything wrong with that decision - E2E tests validate functionality by performing tests from a user’s experience by simulating real user scenarios in the application.

A unit test, on the other hand, is a bit more granular. It’s an automated piece of code that invokes a unit of work (a separate piece of code) in the application and is usually approached from a black-box perspective. The test passes or fails based on an assumption or expectation about the behavior of that unit of work.

🤔 If you’re interested in learning more about writing well-structured and valuable tests in Angular, check out my other article.

A unit test is almost always written using a testing framework, allowing it to be written efficiently and run quickly. If you generate a new Angular project with the Angular CLI, your application comes with Jasmine and Karma (the testing framework and runner) out of the box.

📢 This article assumes that you’re using Jasmine and Karma for your unit testing. However, if you’re using another testing framework like Jest, the approach will be almost identical.

Angular Button testing: Data Validation or Application Functionality

There isn’t a set rule on if buttons should be covered by a unit test. In fact, the decision of whether to write a unit test for a button click ultimately comes down to personal opinion. If you prefer to defer that functionality to an E2E test, that’s great! But in my opinion, there are certain situations where a button click unit test provides valuable reassurance in an Angular application.

Consider the classic calculator example which contains many buttons that perform various mathematical operations. Each time a button is clicked, data is manipulated and a new number or sum is displayed on the screen. This is a perfect scenario for a unit test! Data is changing with each button click; the calculator produces a certain output when given a certain input.

On the other hand, it’s not uncommon for a button to navigate the user to a different page, or to make something else appear or disappear. Rather than solely changing data, these scenarios represent application functionality and is a great opportunity to write an E2E test.

With this in mind, does your situation call for a unit test or would it be best to create an E2E test?

Recall that there are generally two approaches to writing a unit test for buttons: either you locate the button on the DOM and simulate a click, or you test against the code that will be run when the button is clicked by a user. Let’s look at the more complex example first.

How to Test a Button Click in Angular

This approach may be useful in some situations, but the act of delegating a unit test to browse the DOM to find the button and perform a click is a point of contention. The unit test still makes expectations around the notion of what’s supposed to happen when the button is clicked, but many argue that performing the click is the responsibility of an E2E test.

Regardless, locating the button on the DOM is a trivial task, especially when you isolate the button into a reusable component. The following is an example of just that - a reusable isolated button component that, as mentioned prior, assumes you have TestBed configured properly with Jasmine and Karma.

describe('Component: Button', () => { let fixture: ComponentFixture<ButtonComponent>; let component: ButtonComponent; beforeEach(async(() => { TestBed.configureTestingModule({ imports: [ ], declarations: [ ButtonComponent ], providers: [ ] }).compileComponents().then(() => { fixture = TestBed.createComponent(ButtonComponent); component = fixture.componentInstance; }); }));
}); it('should call onButtonClick when clicked', fakeAsync(() => { spyOn(component, 'onButtonClick'); let button = fixture.debugElement.nativeElement.querySelector('button'); button.click(); tick(); expect(component.onButtonClick).toHaveBeenCalled();
}));

The TypeScript file for this button component has a function called onButtonClick that is bound to the button element in the template. This test first spies on the local function, locates the button and then performs a click. After a simulated passage of time with tick(), we make an assertion that the onButtonClick function was called.

📣 Events can be tested using async / fakeAsync function imported from '@angular/core/testing’.

Notice that the button was located on the DOM using querySelector and passing button as an argument. This works fine in an isolated component like this, but in different scenarios where multiple button elements may exist, you need to use something that provides more specificity.

This example is pretty straightforward - we simply verify that the function is called when the button is clicked. But we can take this further. Let’s look at the onButtonClick function and see what else can be tested.

@Output() buttonClicked: EventEmitter<any> = new EventEmitter<any>(); ... onButtonClick(): void { this.buttonClicked.emit();
}

Since this is a reusable button component, it makes sense to delegate the responsibility of functionality to whichever component consumes it. In order for the parent component to identify when the button has been clicked, it can listen to an event emitter inside the button component (buttonClicked). In response for the event being emitted, the parent component calls a local function to, for example, perform a mathematical operation in the calculator example above.

From a testing perspective, it would provide value to have a unit test that ensures that the event is emitted when the onButtonClick function is called. Here’s what that may look like.

it('should emit the event when #onButtonClicked is called', () => { const emitSpy = spyOn(component.buttonClickEvent, 'emit'); component.onButtonClick(); expect(emitSpy).toHaveBeenCalled();
});

Spies come to the rescue here again. The emit function lives on the buttonClickEvent object, and the test simply verifies that the spy was called when the code under test is executed.

What about other situations?

There may be other situations in a reusable button component where unit tests could prove useful and provide reassurance that it’ll continue to work in the future with additional changes. We won’t be discussion or covering those scenarios in this article, though.

How to Test Button Functionality in Angular

Now that the reusable button component has a supporting test cases, let’s turn our attention to situations where it may prove beneficial to test local code that is wired up to that button component.

Recall that the reusable button component emits an event when clicked. Other parts of our application can listen to that event, and call a local function to perform isolated operations.

Continuing our calculator idea from earlier, here’s one example where we consume the reusable button component and listen to the buttonClickEvent.

<app-button (buttonClickEvent)="add(5)"> 5
</app-button>

We already have unit tests that locate the button on the DOM and initiate a click event, so there’s no need to test that here in the parent component. Instead, let’s look directly at the add function and see if there’s anything inside worth testing.

add(toAdd: number) { this.total += toAdd;
}

This is a very basic and straightforward example specifically for this article. This function mutates data, and if you recall from earlier, this is a great opportunity to add supporting test cases.

But what do you test?

For the add function, we would write a test that ensures the total class variable increments with the appropriate value passed to the function. This example is pretty simple, but the skill of determining what to test is something that comes with practice.

📣 If you’re wanting to develop the skill of identifying test cases, consider reading my article titled The Gumball Machine - How To Quickly Identify Unit Test Cases

Here’s what the test would look like. Again this assumes you have the test suite set up correctly with TestBed.

it('should add 5 to the calculator total', () => { const expectedTotal: number = 10; component.total = 5; component.add(5); expect(component.total).toEqual(expectedTotal);
});

Notice that we call the add function directly in the parent component test. Remember, we already have assurance that the button works as intended when clicked, so in this case we simply call the code under test.

⚠️ It’s never good to trust that a test is working as intended without seeing it fail. I recommend making an intentional error in the add function (not the test) to see if the test fails when it needs to fail.

Conclusion

In this article, we investigated the different ways to test button clicks in Angular. One option is to write a unit test that locates the button on the DOM, perform a click and assert that something happened in the test. On the other hand, it may be appropriate to simply call function that is called when the button is clicked and write expectations based on what occurs in the code under test.

Ultimately, it comes down to your personal preference. Whichever route you choose to take, I hope this article has proven helpful and shown you how to write unit tests for button clicks in Angular.
